name: implementing-with-tdd
description: Use when implementing bug fixes, features, or any code changes where test-first development is appropriate.
author: BPSAI
securitySummary: This is a pure prompt-based skill containing only documentation and guidance for test-driven development workflows. The static analyzer flagged documentation text as security issues: 'Hardcoded URL' refers to a metadata source_url field, 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' is a false positive from matching 'TDD' text, and 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' refers to markdown code blocks showing legitimate pytest and ruff commands. No executable code, network requests, or file system modifications exist. Safe to publish.
valueStatement: Writing tests before code ensures reliable, maintainable software. This skill guides developers through TDD cycles including test creation, red-green-refactor workflow, and project-specific testing conventions.
limitations: Does not execute code or tests

useCases.title: Feature implementation
useCases.description: Add new features following TDD methodology with pytest tests

useCases.title: Bug fix verification
useCases.description: Reproduce bugs with failing tests before implementing fixes

useCases.title: Code review preparation
useCases.description: Ensure code changes have comprehensive test coverage

promptTemplates.title: New feature
promptTemplates.prompt: Use implementing-with-tdd skill to add a new feature. Start with task update and write failing tests first.

promptTemplates.title: Bug fix
promptTemplates.prompt: Use implementing-with-tdd skill to fix the bug. Write a test that reproduces the issue before implementing the fix.

promptTemplates.title: Refactoring
promptTemplates.prompt: Use implementing-with-tdd skill to refactor. Ensure tests pass before and after changes using pytest.

promptTemplates.title: Full TDD cycle
promptTemplates.prompt: Guide me through a complete TDD cycle: update task status, write failing test, implement code, verify green, refactor, and complete task.

bestPractices: Always run tests in red-green-refactor order to verify each step
Use descriptive test names following project conventions
Run the full CI suite (bpsai-pair ci) before marking complete
Mock external services in unit tests to ensure isolation
antiPatterns: Writing implementation code before tests (violates TDD)
Skipping test execution and assuming code works
Leaving failing tests in the codebase
Modifying tests to pass instead of fixing the implementation

faq.question: What tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: Works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Provides commands for pytest, ruff, and bpsai-pair CLI.

faq.question: How long should a TDD cycle take?
faq.answer: Keep cycles short. Write one failing test, implement minimum code, verify pass. Typical cycle is 5-15 minutes.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with CI/CD?
faq.answer: Yes. Run bpsai-pair ci locally before pushing. It executes tests, linting, and type checks.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: This is a prompt-based skill with no code execution. It only provides guidance for manual workflows.

faq.question: What if tests fail unexpectedly?
faq.answer: Run pytest --lf to re-run only failed tests. Use pytest -x to stop on first failure for debugging.

faq.question: How is this different from other testing skills?
faq.answer: This skill is specific to PairCoder project conventions with custom commands, fixtures, and task workflow integration.
